title: Dream Demotion (active move that fell below baseline)
description: One demotion event from the moves substrate's quality-control pass (dream Phase 6c). Written by `run_demotion_pass` to `~/.trinity/dream_demotions.jsonl` (one row per line) when an active move's posterior drifts below its T4 threshold. The move's full state at demotion is preserved on disk at `~/.trinity/moves/archive/<slug>/SKILL.md`; this log is the time-indexed event stream — useful for analytics ('how often does the gate retire moves?') and for sanity-checking that demotions correlate with intended user signal. Append-only; nothing in Trinity reads it for routing.

move_slug.description: Kebab-case slug from `_slugify(move.name)`. Matches the directory name at `~/.trinity/moves/archive/<slug>/`.

move_name.description: The Move's original `name` (human-readable). Stored alongside the slug so analytics don't have to re-resolve.

move_basin.description: Basin id (from `topics.json`) the move served. Null when the move wasn't basin-anchored.

demoted_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp (seconds precision) when the demotion fired.

tier.description: Which tier rejected the move on re-eval. T4 = Beta-Binomial posterior fell below baseline (the only tier that ships today). T1/T2/T3 re-eval is planned but not yet wired.

reason.description: Tier-specific reason from the failing `TierResult.reason`. Typically a one-line summary of the score vs threshold.

posterior.description: The posterior value at demotion (alpha / (alpha + beta)). Null when the demoting tier wasn't T4. Surfaced separately so analytics don't need to reconstruct it from alpha/beta.

baseline.description: The trinity_eval_baseline the posterior was compared against. Null when no baseline existed (first-eval demotions).

executions.description: Total chairman councils this move was applied to before demotion (alpha + beta - 2, excluding the uninformative prior). Lets analytics compute the average lifetime of a promoted move.
